words.name: Annoyance Test Republik
words.description: Tactic: deliberately irritate the publishers to trigger an open-source implementation of one-click-hide

words.name: One-Click-Hide
words.description: Feature that hides a contribution via a single reversible click

words.name: Plato Goal
words.description: Transmission of the idea to the next generation as the actual addressee, not majority persuasion

words.name: P(publishers implement | annoyed)
words.description: Probability that publishers enable the feature in response to irritation

words.name: P(publishers throttle | annoyed)
words.description: Probability of the competing cheaper reaction: ignore or limit the user

words.name: Implementation Cost
words.description: Effort for publishers, given an open-source PR is supplied by Timon

words.name: Single-Attempt Failure Rate
words.description: Share of individual tactics in a portfolio that fail on their own

words.name: Bus Factor zukunft.com
words.description: Number of people whose loss would halt the project

values.note_ref: Assumption: PR is mergeable and reversible; real cost includes review, UX, abuse vetting
Claude's estimate; revisable upward given the open-source PR, since building is no longer the most expensive option
Competing cheaper reaction; Moos supplies the justification for it free of charge
Timon's own estimate: very high failure probability per single attempt, hence a portfolio is required
Father-son transmission nominally raises this to 2 but remains structurally a single point of failure until an external collaborator joins
